channel.title: Comments for page "Nor Shall My Sword Sleep"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Nor Shall My Sword Sleep"

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Given this origin, and that the original sources of this image cannot be accessed, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: good shit op

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh bloody hell. This is fantastic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edward VII was dead by 1919; the King of Britain was George V at this time. Or are you suggestion that the Foundation somehow extended his life?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really appreciated the spoiler by SRegan, since I tried to read this for like five times, had no clue, and then i stumbled upon the comments.
I would only repeat what others have said. I like this, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wonderful, simply wonderful.

I love the way you've had the Foundation adapt to the change in circumstances. And they assume that they are normal and everything around them is anomaly? That is such perfect human nature. Like a drunk person demanding that you stop wobbling all over the place and stand still.

"Hey! Stop being so giant so I can hit you!"
"Mini-Foundation, go home, your shrunk."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Voldemort. It's okay, you can say his name, he's dead.

No, I'm assuming Dark. He's previously been seen to interact with Marshall and Carter via letters from the past.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is beautiful. There's only one thing I'm curious about… who or what exactly is the "You-Know-Who" referenced in the beginning italics section?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderfully done. I can understand why they are so pissed at us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohh okay. I havent read all the -001 articles yet, Ill have to give Gears's a once-over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fridge horror about people conceived after 1903 or people who were fetuses/infants at the time really made this for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, now that's clever. :shamelessly pilfers: My original thinking was that I would have the skips found roughly in numerical order, so I could make use of any skip numbered under 148. However, on second thoughts I'm assuming 148 was numbered before 001 (1911 in my head-chronology), so this makes a lot of sense and fits well - thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only nitpick is that I would have gone with #45393BE-048 instead of #45393BE-148.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some bits that grate on me, but otherwise this is really well done, and it reminds me vaguely of "The Peshawar Lancers".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Genius. Absolute genius. Alternate reality scips are some of my favourites

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost think that "purge" would be better than "punish"in this context. Upvote regardless!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It took me reading the entire thing to get what it pertained to, but I did figure it out. Brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is beautifully written. It summons a lot in a short amount of time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is. I'm sort of trying to tie together a headcanon (based on "From the desk of B Dark" by pooryoric) whereby Dark has some sort of skip that lets him communicate with his past self, thereby turning himself into a living (and later not-so-living) paradox engine. As a consequence, even though he's dead stacks of letters from future versions of him keep turning up, but their predictions are becoming progressively less accurate as the versions of him that are still alive get further and further from the main timeline.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the "Marshall" at the heading of the article supposed to be the same from MC&D?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheers. I'm trying to keep in with the 001 proposal he came from - there Hermann Keter was presumably killed by 001 in 1911; here the point of divergence is before its discovery, so he survives and the Safe-Euclid-Keter system is never adopted. The figure is actually a blurred, desaturated stock image of a lab tech reaching down through a fumigation hood, which I thought worked well with the premise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I caught what was going on here before I read the spoilers, and I love this with every inch of my heart

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Oh. This is just delightful. Two thumbs up and a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still say "H. Keter" is cheesy for a name in a tale related to the foundation, but now it's growing on me XD. This is one of my favorite SCiPs, so seeing it used so well in a tale is great. I'm curious to know where you found that blurry image for the figure in the sky, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was like "what the… I don't even…" while/after reading, then upvoted when I realized what's this all about by reading the first comment.
Actually, this is a pretty creative idea with great execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it

The only change I would make would be to add more paragraph breaks, as it looks a bit wall-of-texty on my mobile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something I've been working on for a while - I considered rewriting as a series of vignettes, but feedback on the forums and IRC persuaded me otherwise.

Spoilers
Close

It's the microcosmic Foundation within SCP-1235. The formatting is taken from Gears' 001 proposal, which is intended to represent how the Foundation catalogued items before the Safe-Euclid-Keter system. Similarly, it doesn't use SI units. From the perspective of the microcosmos, history played out normally until March 1st 1903, when the universe vanished and was replaced by a macroverse. History following the appearance of the macroverse is obviously divergent, which I've hopefully made nods to with 'Curie devices' and similar. The last digits of the skips referenced refer to the corresponding numbers on the main list - what seems to be an area code in Gears' 001 proposal reflects wherever possible where the skips in question were found. I had good luck with them being recovered before the point of divergence until I hit 047, which is stated to have been recovered in the 1990s. For reference. I intend this to be a similar item but not necessarily the same one - it's the item they used to modify macrocosmic virii in the early 1235 containment breaches.
